item.title: CVE-2026-46050 md/raid10: fix deadlock with check operation and nowait requests
CVE-2026-46011 media: mtk-jpeg: fix use-after-free in release path due to uncancelled work
CVE-2026-45877 HID: intel-ish-hid: fix NULL-ptr-deref in ishtp_bus_remove_all_clients
CVE-2026-45917 ipvs: do not keep dest_dst if dev is going down
CVE-2026-45841 netfilter: nfnetlink_osf: fix divide-by-zero in OSF_WSS_MODULO
CVE-2026-46062 ntfs3: fix integer overflow in run_unpack() volume boundary check
CVE-2026-46005 xfs: fix a resource leak in xfs_alloc_buftarg()
CVE-2026-45930 net: mctp: ensure our nlmsg responses are initialised
CVE-2026-46021 thermal: core: Fix thermal zone governor cleanup issues
CVE-2026-46037 ipv4: icmp: validate reply type before using icmp_pointers
CVE-2026-46084 RDMA/mana_ib: Disable RX steering on RSS QP destroy
CVE-2026-46012 rxrpc: Fix memory leaks in rxkad_verify_response()
CVE-2026-46085 rxrpc: Fix rxkad crypto unalignment handling
CVE-2026-46059 KVM: nSVM: Always use NextRIP as vmcb02's NextRIP after first L2 VMRUN
CVE-2026-46004 ALSA: caiaq: Handle probe errors properly
CVE-2026-45901 netfilter: nf_tables: revert commit_mutex usage in reset path
CVE-2026-46080 ocfs2: split transactions in dio completion to avoid credit exhaustion
CVE-2026-45894 iommu/vt-d: Clear Present bit before tearing down PASID entry
CVE-2026-45840 openvswitch: cap upcall PID array size and pre-size vport replies
CVE-2026-46054 selinux: fix overlayfs mmap() and mprotect() access checks
CVE-2026-45991 udf: fix partition descriptor append bookkeeping
CVE-2026-46027 net/smc: avoid early lgr access in smc_clc_wait_msg
CVE-2026-46088 ALSA: control: Validate buf_len before strnlen() in snd_ctl_elem_init_enum_names()
CVE-2026-46051 md/raid5: fix soft lockup in retry_aligned_read()
CVE-2026-46053 net: rds: fix MR cleanup on copy error
CVE-2026-46018 ALSA: usb-audio: stop parsing UAC2 rates at MAX_NR_RATES
CVE-2026-45835 Bluetooth: L2CAP: Fix null-ptr-deref in l2cap_sock_new_connection_cb()
CVE-2026-45834 Bluetooth: L2CAP: Fix null-ptr-deref in l2cap_sock_state_change_cb()
CVE-2026-45944 iommu/vt-d: Clear Present bit before tearing down context entry
CVE-2026-45932 bpf: Fix tcx/netkit detach permissions when prog fd isn't given
CVE-2026-45836 Bluetooth: L2CAP: Fix null-ptr-deref in l2cap_sock_get_sndtimeo_cb()
CVE-2026-45961 gfs2: fix memory leaks in gfs2_fill_super error path
CVE-2026-44844 eml_parser: Recursion DoS via nested message/rfc822 attachments
CVE-2026-45839 bpf: reject negative CO-RE accessor indices in bpf_core_parse_spec()
CVE-2026-45940 net: stmmac: fix oops when split header is enabled
CVE-2026-44708 Mistune Math Plugin XSS Escape Bypass
CVE-2026-44897 Mistune Heading ID Attribute Injection XSS
CVE-2026-45893 apparmor: Fix & Optimize table creation from possibly unaligned memory
CVE-2026-45943 erofs: fix inline data read failure for ztailpacking pclusters
CVE-2026-46017 mm: fix deferred split queue races during migration
CVE-2026-45897 netfilter: nft_counter: serialize reset with spinlock
CVE-2026-45997 scsi: sd: fix missing put_disk() when device_add(&disk_dev) fails
CVE-2026-45986 crypto: ccree - fix a memory leak in cc_mac_digest()
CVE-2026-47104 libusb < 1.0.30 Out-of-Bounds Read in parse_iad_array()
CVE-2026-46047 net: qrtr: ns: Fix use-after-free in driver remove()
CVE-2026-45571 go-git: Crafted repositories may modify main and submodule .git directories
CVE-2026-45850 ipvs: skip ipv6 extension headers for csum checks
CVE-2026-46052 ceph: only d_add() negative dentries when they are unhashed
CVE-2026-46009 PCI: endpoint: pci-epf-ntb: Remove duplicate resource teardown
CVE-2026-46070 md/raid5: validate payload size before accessing journal metadata
CVE-2026-46043 RDMA/rxe: Validate pad and ICRC before payload_size() in rxe_rcv
CVE-2026-45994 ibmasm: fix OOB reads in command_file_write due to missing size checks
CVE-2026-46069 wifi: mwifiex: fix use-after-free in mwifiex_adapter_cleanup()
CVE-2026-45859 netfilter: nfnetlink_queue: do shared-unconfirmed check before segmentation
CVE-2026-46032 KVM: nSVM: Triple fault if restore host CR3 fails on nested #VMEXIT
CVE-2026-46101 netfilter: reject zero shift in nft_bitwise
CVE-2026-46014 KVM: SVM: Add missing save/restore handling of LBR MSRs
CVE-2026-45845 net/sched: taprio: fix NULL pointer dereference in class dump
CVE-2026-46086 net: bridge: use a stable FDB dst snapshot in RCU readers
CVE-2026-46065 fbdev: defio: Disconnect deferred I/O from the lifetime of struct fb_info
CVE-2026-46098 net: caif: clear client service pointer on teardown
CVE-2026-45861 gfs2: Fix slab-use-after-free in qd_put
CVE-2026-46077 crypto: atmel-tdes - fix DMA sync direction
CVE-2026-46063 x86/shstk: Prevent deadlock during shstk sigreturn
CVE-2026-46056 Bluetooth: hci_event: fix potential UAF in SSP passkey handlers
CVE-2026-45956 drm/exynos: vidi: use priv->vidi_dev for ctx lookup in vidi_connection_ioctl()
CVE-2026-46068 crypto: nx - fix bounce buffer leaks in nx842_crypto_{alloc,free}_ctx
CVE-2026-45843 slip: bound decode() reads against the compressed packet length
CVE-2026-46024 libceph: Prevent potential null-ptr-deref in ceph_handle_auth_reply()
CVE-2026-45963 ASoC: nau8821: Cancel delayed work on component remove
CVE-2026-45998 rxrpc: Fix potential UAF after skb_unshare() failure
CVE-2026-46023 dm mirror: fix integer overflow in create_dirty_log()
CVE-2026-45844 netfilter: arp_tables: fix IEEE1394 ARP payload parsing
CVE-2026-45892 ext4: drop extent cache after doing PARTIAL_VALID1 zeroout
CVE-2026-46022 misc: ibmasm: fix OOB MMIO read in ibmasm_handle_mouse_interrupt()
CVE-2026-46102 net: strparser: fix skb_head leak in strp_abort_strp()
CVE-2026-46016 remoteproc: xlnx: Only access buffer information if IPI is buffered
CVE-2026-46000 rxrpc: Fix conn-level packet handling to unshare RESPONSE packets
CVE-2025-71305 drm/display/dp_mst: Add protection against 0 vcpi
CVE-2026-46006 drm/nouveau: fix u32 overflow in pushbuf reloc bounds check
CVE-2026-46003 net: qrtr: ns: Limit the total number of nodes
CVE-2026-46048 ALSA: caiaq: fix usb_dev refcount leak on probe failure
CVE-2026-46002 ext2: reject inodes with zero i_nlink and valid mode in ext2_iget()
CVE-2026-46078 erofs: fix the out-of-bounds nameoff handling for trailing dirents
CVE-2026-46064 ibmasm: fix heap over-read in ibmasm_send_i2o_message()
CVE-2026-46075 crypto: atmel-sha204a - Fix potential UAF and memory leak in remove path
CVE-2026-45973 RDMA/mlx5: Fix UMR hang in LAG error state unload
CVE-2026-45838 bpf: fix end-of-list detection in cgroup_storage_get_next_key()
CVE-2026-45899 ext4: drop extent cache when splitting extent fails
CVE-2026-46071 KVM: nSVM: Avoid clearing VMCB_LBR in vmcb12
CVE-2026-46049 ALSA: ctxfi: Add fallback to default RSR for S/PDIF
CVE-2026-46066 ceph: fix num_ops off-by-one when crypto allocation fails
CVE-2026-45989 of: unittest: fix use-after-free in testdrv_probe()
CVE-2026-45855 ata: libata-scsi: avoid Non-NCQ command starvation
CVE-2026-46058 media: amphion: Fix race between m2m job_abort and device_run
CVE-2026-46031 net: ks8851: Reinstate disabling of BHs around IRQ handler
CVE-2026-45912 ext4: don't cache extent during splitting extent
CVE-2026-45999 erofs: fix unsigned underflow in z_erofs_lz4_handle_overlap()
CVE-2026-44896 Mistune: XSS via unescaped figclass/figwidth in Figure directive
CVE-2026-46091 media: rc: igorplugusb: heed coherency rules
CVE-2026-45958 drm/exynos: vidi: fix to avoid directly dereferencing user pointer
CVE-2026-45846 bareudp: fix NULL pointer dereference in bareudp_fill_metadata_dst()
CVE-2026-44899 Mistune Image Directive CSS Injection Vulnerability
CVE-2026-44898 Mistune TOC Anchor Injection XSS
CVE-2026-46089 zram: do not forget to endio for partial discard requests
CVE-2026-46033 crypto: authencesn - reject short ahash digests during instance creation
CVE-2026-46046 ext4: fix missing brelse() in ext4_xattr_inode_dec_ref_all()
CVE-2026-23679 libusb < 1.0.30 NULL Pointer Dereference in parse_interface()
CVE-2026-45570 go-git: Improper single-quote escaping in go-git SSH transport
CVE-2026-46038 net: qrtr: ns: Free the node during ctrl_cmd_bye()
CVE-2026-46040 inotify: fix watch count leak when fsnotify_add_inode_mark_locked() fails
CVE-2026-45988 rxrpc: Fix re-decryption of RESPONSE packets
CVE-2026-45996 spi: imx: fix use-after-free on unbind
CVE-2026-45942 ext4: fix e4b bitmap inconsistency reports
CVE-2026-46019 crypto: atmel-aes - Fix 3-page memory leak in atmel_aes_buff_cleanup
CVE-2026-46103 can: ucan: fix devres lifetime
CVE-2026-46092 wifi: rtw88: check for PCI upstream bridge existence
CVE-2026-45842 slip: reject VJ receive packets on instances with no rstate array
CVE-2026-45949 hwrng: core - use RCU and work_struct to fix race condition
CVE-2026-46044 ipmi:ssif: Clean up kthread on errors
CVE-2026-46072 ntfs3: add buffer boundary checks to run_unpack()
CVE-2026-46079 rbd: fix null-ptr-deref when device_add_disk() fails
CVE-2026-46099 net: ipv6: fix NOREF dst use in seg6 and rpl lwtunnels
CVE-2026-46083 spi: fix resource leaks on device setup failure
CVE-2026-45987 KVM: nSVM: Sync interrupt shadow to cached vmcb12 after VMRUN of L2
CVE-2026-46015 tcp: call sk_data_ready() after listener migration
CVE-2026-45858 ext4: don't zero the entire extent if EXT4_EXT_DATA_PARTIAL_VALID1
CVE-2026-45934 btrfs: fix EEXIST abort due to non-consecutive gaps in chunk allocation
CVE-2026-46090 ALSA: aloop: Fix peer runtime UAF during format-change stop
CVE-2026-46082 KVM: SVM: Inject #UD for INVLPGA if EFER.SVME=0
CVE-2026-45993 LoongArch: Add spectre boundry for syscall dispatch table
CVE-2026-46026 net: qrtr: ns: Limit the maximum number of lookups
CVE-2026-46076 KVM: nSVM: Raise #UD if unhandled VMMCALL isn't intercepted by L1
CVE-2026-46094 ext4: fix bounds check in check_xattrs() to prevent out-of-bounds access
Employees are unknowingly inviting tech support impersonators into firms, says FBI
item.description: Online or telephone IT support scams have been tricking employees into downloading or clicking on malware for years. But according to the FBI, one group that targets US-based law firms has recently found success in person, by convincing firms to allow a supposed IT support person…

item.title: Graduation day grifts
The bipartisan case for CISA.
item.description: This week, Dave and Ben sit down to discuss a growing bipartisan effort to support CISA. Throughout the conversation, the two look at how lawmakers from both sides of the aisle are showing greater support for CISA after the Trump administration cut its budget and workforce. Both …

item.title: Companies built AI into core systems before figuring out how to govern it
item.description: 70% of organizations use GenAI in live environments, and 64% have AI agents in pilot or production deployments. Some of those agents have privileged access to core systems, according to Check Point’s 2026 Cloud Security Report. Confirmed and suspected AI incidents (Source: Check …

item.title: Canonical releases Workshop for one-command sandboxed dev environments on Ubuntu
item.description: Canonical released Workshop, a tool that launches sandboxed development environments on Ubuntu with a single command. Environments are configured once and reproduced on different machines, giving teams consistent setups across development workstations and deployment pipelines. A …

item.title: Hottest cybersecurity open-source tools of the month: May 2026
item.description: Presented here is a curated selection of noteworthy open-source cybersecurity solutions that have drawn recognition for their ability to enhance security postures across diverse settings. Pipelock: Open-source AI agent firewall AI coding agents run with shell access, environment …

item.title: Kemper - 269,299 breached accounts
item.description: In April 2026, the American insurance holding company Kemper Corporation was named by the ShinyHunters ransomware group in a "pay or leak" extortion campaign . The attackers allegedly accessed Kemper's Salesforce environment via social engineering as part of a broader campaign ta…

item.title: Ketch brings multi-agent AI orchestration to enterprise privacy programs
item.description: Ketch has unveiled its vision for agentic privacy with the Ketch Agent Network, a multi-agent orchestration layer for enterprise privacy programs. The platform is designed to continuously reason across legal obligations, internal policies, and operational realities within a unifi…

item.title: The CISO selling confidence in a market full of breach headlines
item.description: Engineering teams across enterprise IT are writing their own software with AI coding assistants, spinning up agents that act on their behalf, and assigning those agents the same access privileges their human creators hold. The shift has pulled the role of the chief information se…

item.title: Nordic CISOs Handle Rising Cyber Threats Remarkably Well
item.description: Artificial intelligence notwithstanding, the vast majority of CISOs in northern Europe say they're facing no more serious cyberattacks than they did two years ago.

item.title: XM Cyber enhances identity risk visibility with continuous exposure management capabilities
item.description: XM Cyber has announced platform enhancements aimed at helping organizations reduce identity risk, compounded by AI-enabled attackers. According to Gartner, “By 2028, 70% of CISOs will use identity visibility and intelligence capabilities to shrink the IAM attack surface, reducing…

item.title: Police arrest suspect in Ajax football club hack that exposed 300,000 fan records
item.description: The Dutch National Police arrested a man suspected of hacking into the computer systems of AFC Ajax, a football club from Amsterdam. “On the morning of Tuesday, May 26, detectives arrested a 35-year-old man from the municipality of Buren for computer intrusion at the Amsterdam fo…

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 28th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9948, (Thu, May 28th)
item.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

item.title: Nudge Security adds browser-based discovery for shadow AI agents
item.description: Nudge Security announced that its AI security platform offers discovery of shadow AI agents via the browser, extending its agent discovery capabilities to cover platforms that do not provide a public API for agent identity and inventory. The new browser-based agentic AI discovery…

item.title: Frontier AI models collapse under multi-turn AI attacks, Cisco finds
item.description: Attackers who probe large language models rarely give up after one refusal. They reframe, build context across turns, adopt personas, and escalate gradually. New research from Cisco’s AI threat intelligence team finds that the safety benchmarks used across the industry miss…

item.title: Checksum introduces Continuous Quality Agent for automated test generation and healing
item.description: Checksum has launched its Continuous Quality Agent, an autonomous system that runs nightly against deployed applications and automatically heals broken tests without waiting for an engineer to open a dashboard or write a prompt. AI coding has changed the constraint in software de…

item.title: JINX-0164 Targets Cryptocurrency Firms with Fake Recruiter Lures and macOS Malware
item.description: A new campaign orchestrated by a previously undocumented threat actor has targeted cryptocurrency organizations with an aim to facilitate digital asset theft using recruitment-themed social engineering and bespoke macOS malware. "These campaigns leveraged sophisticated social eng…

item.title: Pirates in the crosshairs: how one cybercrime gang has been infecting book, movie, and TV show fans for years
item.description: Our experts continue to track attacks targeting consumers of pirated content, both books and movies. 2026 saw the discovery of new target sites with tens of millions of visitors, while the miner gained a RAT module.

item.title: Inside the customer environment: Where threat actors, vulnerabilities, and exposed assets intersect
item.description: Tenable Research has developed a graph-based model linking 600+ threat groups to real-world customer exposures. It reveals which vulnerabilities sit at the intersection of severity, active exploitation, and organizational risk. Key takeaways The "patch everything" strategy is dea…

item.title: CISA Orders Emergency Drupal Patch | Microsoft Server Bug | Google Fights Canada Surveillance Bill
item.description: CISA has ordered U.S. federal civilian agencies to urgently patch an actively exploited critical Drupal SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-9082) affecting PostgreSQL-backed Drupal deployments, after Imperva reported more than 15,000 attack attempts across 65 countries. Microso…

item.title: CVE-2026-43503 net: skbuff: propagate shared-frag marker through frag-transfer helpers
CVE-2026-46300 net: skbuff: preserve shared-frag marker during coalescing
CVE-2026-41401 libyang - Heap Use-After-Free Write in XML Metadata Parsing

CVE-2026-39824 Invoking integer overflow in NewNTUnicodeString in golang.org/x/sys/windows
CVE-2026-42502 Invoking incorrect handling of HTML elements in foreign content in golang.org/x/net/html
CVE-2026-27136 Invoking duplicate attributes can cause XSS in golang.org/x/net/html
CVE-2026-25681 Invoking incorrect handling of character references in DOCTYPE nodes in golang.org/x/net/html
CVE-2026-39829 Invoking pathological RSA/DSA parameters may cause DoS in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
CVE-2026-39830 Invoking client can cause server deadlock on unexpected responses in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
CVE-2026-46597 Invoking byte arithmetic causes underflow and panic in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
CVE-2026-39831 Invoking bypass of FIDO/U2F security keys physical interaction in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
CVE-2026-39827 Invoking memory leak when rejecting channels can lead to DoS in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
CVE-2026-39835 Invoking server panic during CheckHostKey/Authenticate in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
CVE-2026-39834 Invoking infinite loop on large channel writes in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
CVE-2026-39828 Invoking bypass of certificate restrictions in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
CVE-2026-46598 Invoking pathological inputs can lead to client panic in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh/agent
CVE-2026-46595 Invoking VerifiedPublicKeyCallback permissions skip enforcement in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh
CVE-2026-39833 Invoking key constraints not enforced in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh/agent
CVE-2026-42508 Invoking auth bypass via unenforced @revoked status in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh/knownhosts
CVE-2026-9150 Libsolv: stack-based buffer overflow in libsolv's debian metadata parser when handling sha384/sha512 checksums
CVE-2026-9149 Libsolv: heap buffer overflow in libsolv repo_add_solv via negative maxsize from crafted .solv file
CVE-2026-9256 NGINX ngx_http_rewrite_module vulnerability
CVE-2026-6402 webpack-dev-server vulnerable to cross-origin source code exposure on non-HTTPS origins
CVE-2026-5222 Cargo can be coerced to share credentials between registries
CVE-2026-8376 Perl versions through 5.43.10 have a heap buffer overflow when compiling regular expressions with a repeated fixed string on 32-bit builds
CVE-2026-39821 Invoking failure to reject ASCII-only Punycode-encoded labels in golang.org/x/net/idna
CVE-2026-25680 Invoking denial of service when parsing arbitrary HTML in golang.org/x/net/html
CVE-2026-39832 Invoking agent constraints dropped when forwarding keys in golang.org/x/crypto/ssh/agent
CVE-2026-8466 Unbounded buffer accumulation in multipart header parsing causes denial of service in cowboy
CVE-2026-5223 Crates in third party registries can override the cached source of other crates
The NSA, ‘Mythos’ and the quiet emergence of AI cyber doctrine
item.description: For most of my career running security operations, the shape of cyber conflict has been defined by who could move faster than the other side. Faster at identifying a vulnerability, faster at patching, faster at detecting, faster at responding. The last few months have made me ree…

item.title: Gitea Vulnerability Exposes Private Container Images without Authentication
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security flaw in Gitea, an open-source, self-hosted platform for version control, that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to pull private container images from Gitea deployments without requiring an account, password, or other crede…

item.title: Claude now reviews and fixes vulnerabilities as you write code
item.description: Anthropic introduced a security-guidance plugin for Claude Code that reviews code changes for common vulnerabilities and helps Claude identify and fix issues during the same development session. The company says the plugin is designed to catch issues such as injection flaws, unsa…

item.title: FastAPI-based AI tools exposed to authentication bypass by flaw in Starlette framework
item.description: A single malformed character in a web request can let an unauthenticated attacker slip past the access controls that guard applications built on Starlette, the open-source Python framework that powers FastAPI, researchers said. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-48710 could allow atta…

item.title: CISA orders federal agencies to patch actively exploited cPanel plugin flaw within 4 days
item.description: The US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has ordered federal agencies to patch a critical vulnerability in the LiteSpeed cPanel plugin that is being actively exploited in attacks. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-48172, affects the LiteSpeed cPanel user-end plu…

item.title: Microsoft previews automatic device isolation in Defender for Endpoint
item.description: Microsoft is previewing a new automatic device isolation capability in Defender for Endpoint’s auto attack disruption tool to help security pros contain cyber attacks in progress on their IT networks. The company announced the capability earlier this month in a column about new f…

item.title: European AI adoption hits 99% with regulated data driving most policy violations
item.description: Generative AI tools operate inside nearly every European workplace, embedded in meeting transcription services, writing assistants, coding copilots, and search features. Workers in the region pull these tools into daily routines that involve customer records, financial informatio…

item.title: Risky Business #839 -- TeamPCP stole GitHub's internal repos
item.description: On this week’s show Patrick Gray, Adam Boileau and James Wilson discuss the week’s cybersecurity news. They cover: TeamPCP breached GitHub’s internal repos. Now what? Some absolute plonker glued Coruna to a hijacked npm package CISA is worried about about open source and wants th…

item.title: Vigolium: Open-source vulnerability scanner
item.description: Vigolium, an open-source vulnerability scanner that combines deterministic scanning with AI-driven auditing, launched its initial open-source release this month. The project ships 235+ scanner modules and an in-process agent runtime called olium that handles autonomous endpoint d…

item.title: Mytheresa - 84,108 breached accounts
item.description: In April 2026, the luxury fashion e-commerce platform Mytheresa was listed as a victim of the ShinyHunters "pay or leak" extortion group . After the ransom deadline passed, the group publicly released the data which contained 84k unique email addresses. The exposed data also incl…

item.title: CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Zero-Day
item.description: Resolved last week, the vulnerability was exploited in the wild as a zero-day to execute scripts with root privileges. The post CISA Urges Immediate Patching of Exploited LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Novee’s Agentic Fix turns validated exploits into fixes through AI coding agents
item.description: Novee has announced Agentic Fix, an enhancement to its AI penetration testing platform that helps teams move from validating security findings to deploying fixes in a single step. Agentic Fix extends Novee’s platform by generating remediation guidance from the same exploit contex…

item.title: Jetico expands BestCrypt Data Shelter with zero-trust file access controls
item.description: Jetico has announced the extension of BestCrypt Data Shelter to include centrally managed enterprise data access control for sensitive files. The solution allows security teams to define and enforce policies governing which applications, processes and users can access protected f…

item.title: Proofpoint Introduces Active Exploits Protection to Help Organizations Prioritize Vulnerability Patching for Real-World Attacks in the AI Era
CISA gives feds 4 days to patch actively exploited cPanel plugin flaw
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has given U.S. federal agencies four days to secure their servers against a critical vulnerability in the LiteSpeed cPanel user-end plugin, which is actively being exploited in attacks. [...]
